title: Western Blot - Frozen cell pellets
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bakoo","text":"This protocol describes how to extract protein from frozen pellets and how to perform a western blot for protein detection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Thaw the cell pellets On ice .
Prepare gel samples: Mix the appropriate amount of sample with 4x loading dye (LDS), 10x reducing agent and water. Boil the samples for 00:05:00 at 95 °C . Spin briefly.
Add RIPA buffer supplemented with protease inhibitors, vortex well and lyse the cells on ice for 00:30:00 . (For a 200,000 cells pellet use 40 µL of lysis buffer, scale up or down accordingly)
Carefully transfer the supernatant to a new tube. Store the protein lysate at -80 °C or continue with the steps in the Western Blot section.
Run the gel: Load the boiled samples in a pre-made 4-12% Tris-glycine SDS-PAGE gel. Load a well with a prestained ladder. Run at 200V in 1x MES SDS running buffet for 00:45:00 at Room temperature .

Transfer to membrane: Transfer the proteins to a PVDF membrane using the Transblot-Turbo Transfer system (BioRad) following manufacturer's instructions. Check the ladder has transferred well to the membrane.

Blocking: Cut the membrane if the size of the proteins to be detected is different enough. Place the membrane in a cuvette making sure the protein side is facing up. Briefly rinse the membrane with Tris-buffer saline with 0,1% Tween (TBST). Block with 5% skimmed milk-TBST for 01:00:00 at Room temperature or Overnight at 4 °C .

Antibody incubation: Dilute primary antibody in 5% skimmed milk-TBST. Incubate on a shaker Overnight at 4 °C . For abundant targets, incubate for 01:00:00 at Room temperature . Briefly rinse the membrane with TBST. Wash three times with TBST on a shaker for 00:10:00 at Room temperature . Dilute species-appropriate HRP-conjugated secondary antibody in 5% skimmed milk-TBST. Incubate on a shaker for 01:00:00 at Room temperature . From this point, protect the membrane from light. Briefly rinse the membrane with TBST. Wash three times with TBST on a shaker for 00:10:00 at Room temperature . Wash once with TBS on a shaker for 00:05:00 at Room temperature . NOTE: If using an HRP-conjugated primary antibody, wash thrice and image (skip the secondary antibody step).

Detection: Prepare the detection reagent following manufacturer's instructions. Incubate on a shaker for following manufacturer's instructions. Image with chemiluminescence using an appropriate imager.

Stripping: Use a commercial stripping buffer as per instruction. Briefly rinse the membrane with TBST. Repeat blocking, antibody incubation and detection of the new target (usually the loading control). NOTE: A large proportion of the protein will be lost during the stripping step. Always blot the most abundant target last.
creator: Laura Castilla-Vallmanya
